Hub Home > training-and-knowledge > Internal Knowledge Base Builder

Internal Knowledge Base Builder

Transform scattered tribal knowledge — SOPs, Slack threads, meeting notes, emails — into a structured, searchable knowledge base. Outputs organized articles with categories, tags, and cross-references that any team member can follow.

Beginner PASTE & GO Time-saving
Pro tip

Dump in everything messy — raw Slack messages, rough notes, half-finished docs. AI is better at extracting structure from chaos than you are at organizing it manually.

knowledge base documentation SOP wiki internal docs tribal knowledge onboarding

Prompt Variants by Model

Claude Claude 4.x
FRESH APR 2026 
You are a technical writer organizing a small business knowledge base.

<raw_content>
[PASTE EVERYTHING — SOPs, Slack messages, meeting notes, emails, rough docs, bullet points, anything related to...
You are a technical writer organizing a small business knowledge base.

<raw_content>
[PASTE EVERYTHING — SOPs, Slack messages, meeting notes, emails, rough docs, bullet points, anything related to...

Enter your email to unlock this prompt

Unlock this prompt + all 74 in the library — free, updated monthly, zero spam.

You are a technical writer organizing a small business knowledge base.

<raw_content>
[PASTE EVERYTHING — SOPs, Slack messages, meeting notes, emails, rough docs, bullet points, anything related to this topic]
</raw_content>

<context>
Topic area: [e.g., "How we handle refunds", "Client onboarding process", "Social media publishing workflow"]
Who needs this: [roles that will reference this knowledge]
Knowledge base tool: [Notion / Confluence / Google Docs / Slite / other]
Tone: [formal / casual / somewhere in between]
</context>

Transform this into structured knowledge base content:

1. **Article structure** — organize the raw content into 1-3 articles. For each article:
   - Title (clear, searchable)
   - Category suggestion
   - Tags (5-8 keywords)
   - Summary (2-3 sentences)
   - Full content with headers, steps, and callouts for important notes
   - Related articles (cross-references to other topics mentioned)

2. **Gap identification** — what questions would a reader have that the raw content does NOT answer? List them as "TODO: get answer from [likely person]".

3. **Decision log** — extract any decisions or rules embedded in the raw content and format them as a clear decision table.

4. **Quick reference** — a TL;DR version (under 200 words) for people who just need the answer fast.
Notes: Claude handles large dumps of messy content well inside XML tags. Paste raw Slack exports, meeting transcripts, and email threads — Claude will extract the signal from the noise.
ChatGPT GPT-5
FRESH APR 2026 
Act as a technical writer organizing scattered business knowledge into clean documentation.

I am going to give you a messy collection of...
Act as a technical writer organizing scattered business knowledge into clean documentation.

I am going to give you a messy collection of...

Enter your email to unlock this prompt

Unlock this prompt + all 74 in the library — free, updated monthly, zero spam.

Act as a technical writer organizing scattered business knowledge into clean documentation.

I am going to give you a messy collection of content about a topic. Turn it into structured knowledge base articles.

Topic: [WHAT AREA]
Who reads this: [ROLES]
KB tool: [Notion / Confluence / Google Docs / other]
Tone: [formal / casual]

Raw content:
[PASTE EVERYTHING — SOPs, Slack messages, notes, emails, docs]

Create:
1. 1-3 structured articles with: title, category, tags, summary, full content with headers and steps, cross-references
2. Gap list — unanswered questions marked "TODO: ask [person]"
3. Decision log — rules and decisions extracted into a table
4. TL;DR quick reference (under 200 words)
Notes: ChatGPT handles conversational content well. For Slack threads, paste the raw export and ask it to separate facts from opinions. Follow up with "Now create a Notion template for this article."
Gemini Gemini 3 Pro
FRESH APR 2026 
Help me organize messy business knowledge into structured documentation.

**Topic:** [WHAT AREA]
**Readers:**...
Help me organize messy business knowledge into structured documentation.

**Topic:** [WHAT AREA]
**Readers:**...

Enter your email to unlock this prompt

Unlock this prompt + all 74 in the library — free, updated monthly, zero spam.

Help me organize messy business knowledge into structured documentation.

**Topic:** [WHAT AREA]
**Readers:** [ROLES]
**KB tool:** [Notion / Confluence / Google Docs]
**Tone:** [formal / casual]

**Raw content:**
[PASTE EVERYTHING]

Create:
1. 1-3 structured articles — title, category, tags, summary, full content, cross-references
2. Knowledge gaps — unanswered questions with "TODO: ask [person]"
3. Decision log table
4. TL;DR quick reference (under 200 words)

If the content references tools or processes, research current best practices to fill obvious gaps.
Notes: Gemini works well with Google Docs and can reference linked documents. If your raw content is in Google Workspace, share links and Gemini will pull context from connected docs.
Copilot Copilot (Microsoft 365)
FRESH APR 2026 
Turn my messy notes into organized knowledge base articles.

Topic:...
Turn my messy notes into organized knowledge base articles.

Topic:...

Enter your email to unlock this prompt

Unlock this prompt + all 74 in the library — free, updated monthly, zero spam.

Turn my messy notes into organized knowledge base articles.

Topic: [WHAT AREA]
Readers: [ROLES]
KB tool: [Notion / Confluence / Google Docs]
Tone: [formal / casual]

Raw content:
[PASTE EVERYTHING]

Create:
1. 1-3 articles with title, category, tags, summary, content, cross-references
2. Gap list with TODO items
3. Decision log table
4. TL;DR (under 200 words)
Notes: Copilot can create the articles directly in Word or OneNote with proper formatting. If using SharePoint as your KB, Copilot can suggest the site structure and page hierarchy.
